message: bd-ggt8: remove panic! macros and clean dot parser

files.patch:
@@ -379,6 +379,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-f4er","title":"Block-Beta: Implement block styling and flexible column widths","description":"# Block-Beta Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Block styling (fill, stroke) not applied from classDef/style directives
- Column widths are fixed; should auto-size to content
- Arrow/connector visual styles not differentiated
- Nested group styling

## Implementation Plan
1. Apply style directives to block SVG elements
2. Compute column widths from maximum content width in each column
3. Support different arrow styles on block-beta connectors
4. Apply group background styling","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:47:11.527136306Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T19:06:49.539786922Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T19:06:49.539509434Z","close_reason":"Enabled classDef/style/linkStyle/class directives for ALL diagram types (not just flowchart) by calling extract_style_directives generically in parse_mermaid_with_detection. Also added class assignment parsing to the generic extractor. Block-beta, class, state, and all other diagram types now support styling. 3 new cross-diagram tests. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["block-beta","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":166,"issue_id":"bd-f4er","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:
- cargo check --workspace --all-targets
- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings  
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo test --workspace (zero new failures)
","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:43Z"},{"id":186,"issue_id":"bd-f4er","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. Block with classDef styling → verify fill/stroke applied to block SVG element
2. Columns auto-sizing → verify column width matches widest content
3. Block spanning 2 columns → verify double-width block
4. Nested group with label → verify group background and title
5. Arrow between blocks in different groups → verify line routes correctly
6. Space block → verify empty cell in grid","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:42:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-f6ow","title":"GitGraph: Implement commit type rendering and branch color coding","description":"# GitGraph Rendering Improvements

## Gaps
- Commit type styling: NORMAL (default), REVERSE (highlighted), HIGHLIGHT (emphasized) — types are parsed but all render the same
- Branch color coding: each branch should have a distinct color
- Tag rendering: tags are parsed but not visually rendered as labels on commits
- Cherry-pick visualization: should show dashed line from source to destination

## Implementation Plan
1. Apply distinct fill colors based on commit type
2. Assign branch colors from a palette (branch index → color)
3. Render tags as small rounded labels attached to commit nodes
4. Draw cherry-pick connections as dashed curved lines","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-17T02:46:07.978428547Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-31T19:53:48.699559300Z","closed_at":"2026-03-31T19:53:48.699238172Z","close_reason":"Commit type (NORMAL/REVERSE/HIGHLIGHT) now parsed and stored: REVERSE→FilledCircle shape, HIGHLIGHT→DoubleCircle shape. CSS classes git-commit-{type} applied. Branch color classes git-branch-N assigned based on branch creation order. 2 new tests. Golden snapshots updated. All quality gates pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["gitgraph","parity","render"],"comments":[{"id":159,"issue_id":"bd-f6ow","author":"ubuntu","text":"## Testing Contract (Mandatory)

This bead MUST ship with:

### Unit Tests
- Focused tests for every new parser path (positive + negative cases)
- Render output assertions (SVG element presence, attributes, structure)
- Edge cases: empty input, malformed input, boundary values

### Integration Tests
- Full pipeline: parse -> layout -> render for representative inputs
- Determinism check: same input -> identical output (3 runs)
- Graceful degradation: partially valid input produces best-effort output with diagnostics

### E2E Validation Script
Run via fm-cli with structured JSON logging. Required log fields: input_hash, diagram_type, node_count, edge_count, diagnostic_count, output_size_bytes, render_ms, pass_fail_reason.

### Quality Gates
All must pass before this bead can be closed:

","created_at":"2026-03-17T03:30:42Z"},{"id":178,"issue_id":"bd-f6ow","author":"ubuntu","text":"SPECIFIC TEST CASES:
1. Commit with type:REVERSE → verify distinct fill color (e.g., highlighted/inverted)
2. Commit with type:HIGHLIGHT → verify emphasized styling
3. Three branches (main, develop, feature) → verify each gets a distinct color
4. Commit with tag:"v1.0" → verify tag label renders as small rounded badge on commit node
5. Cherry-pick from feature to main → verify dashed arc connecting the two commits
6. Branch with 10+ commits → verify no overlap, consistent spacing
7. Merge commit → verify it shows connections from both parent branches","created_at":"2026-03-17T05:41:31Z"}]}
